section.heading: Separability
section.chapeau: If a provision of this chapter, or the application of a provision of this chapter to any person or circumstance, is held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, that judgment—

paragraph.chapeau: shall not affect, impair, or invalidate—

subparagraph.content: any other provision of this chapter; or
the application of this chapter to any other person or circumstance; and

paragraph.chapeau: shall be confined in its operation to—

subparagraph.content: the provision of this chapter with respect to which the judgment is rendered; or
the application of the provision of this chapter to each person or circumstance directly involved in the controversy in which the judgment is rendered.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: This chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this division”, meaning div. K of Pub. L. 117–58, Nov. 15, 2021, 135 Stat. 1445, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of division K to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 9501 of this title and Tables.
